pi_items.source: Calculated using vector phasor addition method for unbalanced three-phase system with inputs L1=145A, L2=130A, L3=160A at 0°, -120°, -240° angles. Formula: In = sqrt(A² + B² + C² – AB – AC – BC) yields 25.9807621135A, which rounds to 25.98A.
Tolerance value is a task-specific configuration parameter. While reasonable for electrical engineering measurements, no external source confirms this exact tolerance is correct.
Verified as matching the task description and export script expectations. These are synthetic electrical values chosen for the task.
Verified in setup_task.sh (line 11) and export_result.sh (line 12) - path is consistent across all scripts.
Verified in setup_task.sh (line 12) and export_result.sh (line 29) - path is consistent across all scripts.

Google Play Store listing confirms this app exists and is titled 'Electrical Calculations'. App provides calculations for electrical engineering including current, power, impedance, and other measurements. Available at https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.hsn.electricalcalculations

privileged_info_summary: The neutral current calculation for an unbalanced three-phase system with phase currents of 145A, 130A, and 160A has been independently verified to be 25.98A (precisely 25.9807621135A) using both vector phasor addition and the standard algebraic formula In = sqrt(A² + B² + C² – AB – AC – BC). The synthetic input values (L1=145, L2=130, L3=160) are correctly specified. The app package com.hsn.electricalcalculations is a real application available on Google Play Store that performs electrical engineering calculations. All file paths specified in the task metadata (/sdcard/neutral_current.txt and /sdcard/neutral_result.png) are consistent with the task setup and export scripts.
